verdict_reasoning: ABCB9/TAPL is a half-transporter of the MDR/TAP subfamily that localizes to lysosomal/late-endosomal membranes, translocating peptides from cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. Its topology places the protein body within the endolysosomal membrane, not the plasma membrane. Walking the contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet ecto-pool; no narrow germline/developmental surface display (broadly expressed, testis-enriched but intralysosomal); while lysosomes can undergo exocytosis, ABCB9 has no documented surface display via lysosomal exocytosis specifically; no documented PM minority pool or recycling; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs target ABCB9.
